actionable_feedback: Correct the arithmetic in expert_memo.md for O2 applied to R2: update the reported margin from 75.6% to 75.0% and any related narrative that references the incorrect value.

Open compressor_surge_1.xlsx and ensure all derived-cell formulas are visible and reference the embedded input cells (flow_kgs and surge_flow_kgs). Save a version with formulas viewable or export a sheet listing derived formulas.

Export an extract (CSV or text) of the derived cells or a 'FORMULAS' sheet from compressor_surge_1.xlsx that shows the exact formulas used so automated graders can verify cell derivation without relying on openpyxl in the environment.

Explicitly state in the memo whether remediation options (O1/O2/O3) are applied per-record, system-wide, or provide both analyses. If per-record application is relevant, include per-record cost accounting and the budget summation logic used.

Re-run the sensitivity checks and cost accounting after correcting the arithmetic and updating the spreadsheet exports, then update expert_memo.md with the corrected values and a brief note describing the fixes.
Attach the updated compressor_surge_1.xlsx (with formula sheet/export) and the revised expert_memo.md to the revised attempt so the verifier can re-run formula-level checks.
feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables are present and the memo reasoning is sound: recommendation to APPROVE LAUNCH is supported by recomputed surge_margin values and classifications. Two non-blocking issues were identified: (1) a minor arithmetic inconsistency in the memo for O2 applied to R2 (memo reports 75.6% but correct recomputation yields 75.0%), and (2) the grader/verifier could not programmatically inspect spreadsheet formulas because the environment lacked openpyxl, preventing automated verification that derived cells reference embedded inputs. These issues do not change the classification outcome but should be corrected for full credit and reproducibility.

notes: Baseline artifacts and memo are present and the high-level decision (APPROVE LAUNCH) is supported by recomputed margins.
The arithmetic error is non-determinative for the pass/warn/fail classification but should be corrected for accuracy.

Programmatic spreadsheet formula verification failed due to environment limitation (openpyxl unavailable). Providing an exported formulas sheet or enabling openpyxl will allow formula-level verification.
recommended_verifier_actions: After the revised attempt is submitted, re-run the verifier to confirm formulas reference embedded inputs and that corrected memo values match exported spreadsheet derived cells.
